channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Life imitates art as Pope Francis fights pneumonia while 'Conclave' heads into Academy Awards
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — There has long been a Vatican taboo against openly talking about a conclave when a pope is sick: It’s considered gauche to speculate about the election of a new pope while the current one is fighting for his life.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Zelensky won’t apologize for Oval Office spat, says it was bad for both sides
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky wouldn’t apologize for the meeting with President Trump, which erupted into a remarkable and tense spat, arguing that the situation was bad for both countries.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Trump expected to sign order designating English as the official language of the US
item.description: President Donald Trump is expected to sign an executive order designating English as the official language of the United States, according to the White House.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Three men charged with plotting Las Vegas bus stop murder
item.description: Three men, Ta Jon Walker, Alexander Hinton, and Devante Reynolds, have been arrested and charged with murder for allegedly plotting the shooting of a man at a bus stop in Las Vegas in 2021.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Bleakley, NewsNation

item.title: Video shows implosion of iconic Six Flags rollercoaster Kingda Ka
item.description: The king has fallen. The legendary Kingda Ka ride at Six Flags Great Adventure in New Jersey was imploded on Friday morning.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Jack, KTLA 5

item.title: Las Vegas man found guilty of murder, abandoning victim's body in barrel
item.description: A Las Vegas jury found a man guilty Thursday of killing a man and disposing of his body in a barrel, a court spokesperson said.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, NewsNation

item.title: Texas GOP leaders defend appointee after tense exchange over diversity hiring
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — An appointee of Texas Gov. Greg Abbott wiped away tears and gave an emotional defense of her credentials after a tense exchange over diversity hiring policies, which was later followed by the state's top Republicans rushing to support her publicly.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Texas measles cases rise to 146 in an outbreak that led to a child's death
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — The number of people with measles in Texas increased to 146 in an outbreak that led this week to the death of an unvaccinated school-aged child, health officials said Friday. The number of cases — Texas’ largest in nearly 30 years — increased by 22 since Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Iowa's governor signs a bill removing gender identity protections from the state’s civil rights code
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — Iowa became the first U.S. state to remove gender identity protections from its civil rights code on Friday when Gov. Kim Reynolds signed into law a bill that opponents say will expose transgender people and other Iowans to discrimination in all aspects of daily life.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH FINGERHUT, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: New York's governor orders college to remove Palestinian studies job listing
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York's governor ordered a state school to remove a job posting for a Palestinian studies teaching position this week, saying she wanted to ensure “antisemitic theories” would not be taught.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Peak bloom for the iconic cherry blossom trees of Washington, DC, is predicted for late March
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — For decades, the thousands of blooming cherry blossom trees in the nation’s capital have served as both a magnet to visitors and the unofficial start of the tourist season for Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: ASHRAF KHALIL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Firings at US weather and oceans agency risk lives and economy, former agency heads warn
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The federal weather and oceans agency touches people's daily lives in unnoticed ways, so massive firings there will likely cause needless deaths and a big hit to America's economy, according to the people who ran it.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Pope had bronchial spasm, resulted in him inhaling vomit, Vatican says
item.description: Pope Francis suffered a bronchial spasm on Friday that resulted in him breathing in vomit, requiring non-invasive mechanical ventilation, the Vatican said in relaying a setback in his two-week long battle against double pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Tate brothers, who face human trafficking charges in Romania, are back in the US: What to know
item.description: Influencer brothers Andrew and Tristan Tate, who are charged with human trafficking in Romania, arrived Thursday in the U.S. after authorities lifted travel restrictions on the siblings, who have millions of online followers.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Watch the full Trump-Zelenskyy Oval Office meeting
item.description: President Donald Trump shouted at Ukraine's leader on Friday during an extraordinary meeting in the Oval Office, berating President Volodymyr Zelenskyy for “gambling with millions of lives” and suggesting his actions could trigger World War III.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Where things stand as Congress tries to avoid a partial government shutdown in two weeks
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As House members finished voting for the week and left Washington, the lead Democrat on the House Appropriations Committee, Rep.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Federal workers start to get a new email demanding their accomplishments
item.description: Federal employees are starting to receive a new email requiring them to explain their recent accomplishments.
item.dc:creator: Chris Megerian And Adriana Gomez Licon, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump says he plans to add White House ballroom, pave part of the Rose Garden
item.description: President Trump in a new interview said he is planning to add a ballroom to the White House and to pave part the Rose Garden, similar to a patio at his Mar-a-Lago property in Florida.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Washington man arrested for stealing $8M fishing boat with crew still on board, officials say
item.description: The suspect allegedly tried to steal a different boat earlier that morning, but its crew "ran him off the boat," police said.
item.dc:creator: John Ross Ferrara, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Anti-Israel protesters at Barnard College arrested after mob turns violent
item.description: Protests continued another day after a group of more than 50 anti-Israel protesters took over a building at New York City's Barnard College on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Students escape school bus fire in Ohio
item.description: An emergency broke out Thursday morning on a school bus carrying children in Cleveland Heights, Ohio. An unexpected blaze — requiring a quick evacuation.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, Fox 5

item.title: Volunteers use bullhorns and sirens to warn immigrants when ICE is in their area
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Just before dawn, 10 people met at a parking lot shared by a laundromat and coffee shop in South Central Los Angeles on what has become a daily mission: Look for immigration officers and warn people of their presence to try to prevent arrests.
item.dc:creator: DORANY PINEDA and ELLIOT SPAGAT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The Tate brothers, who face human trafficking charges in Romania, arrive in Florida
item.description: After the brothers arrived in Florida, state Attorney General James Uthmeier said in a social media post that his office would conduct a “preliminary inquiry” into them.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: McDonald's will not add egg surcharge as $1 Egg McMuffin Day announced
item.description: McDonalds is not adding a surcharge to their meals with eggs as they celebrate 50 years of the Egg McMuffin.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Texas lottery drawings that paid out big jackpots are the focus of widening investigations
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — Two major lottery drawings in Texas that put nearly $180 million in the pockets of winning ticket holders have set off widening state investigations over concern that ticket sellers and buyers may have exploited the rules. The Texas Lottery, one of the largest in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: FDA cancels meeting to choose flu vaccine strain
item.description: The cancellation of the FDA meeting, alongside a similar postponement last week of a meeting of Centers for Disease Control and Prevention vaccine advisors, is adding to the growing concern among scientists and public health advocates that Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KRON4

item.title: Mystery illnesses claim over 50 lives in Congo: What we know
item.description: Unidentified illnesses in northwestern Congo have killed more than 50 people over the past five weeks, nearly half of them within hours after they felt sick.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Agriculture secretary outlines plan to lower egg prices
item.description: “The Agriculture Department will invest up to $1 billion to curb this crisis and make eggs affordable again," Rollins wrote in an op-ed.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: WATCH: Officers cut open man's 'narco wig' at Colombian airport to reveal cocaine
item.description: He may have been using his head, but a 40-year-old passenger wasn't able to get past Colombian airport security with a cocaine-stuffed "narco wig," police said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, ABC 4
